content.problemStatement:
You have a parameter KKK.

Consider performing the following action on an array AAA of length NNN **exactly once**:


Choose an integer MMM and then MMM intervals [L1,R1],[L2,R2],‚Ä¶,[LM,RM][L_1, R_1], [L_2, R_2], \ldots, [L_M, R_M][L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč],[L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč],‚Ä¶,[LM‚Äč,RM‚Äč] such that:

1‚Č§Li‚Č§Ri‚Č§N1 \le L_i \le R_i \le N1‚Č§Li‚Äč‚Č§Ri‚Äč‚Č§N
L1=1L_1 = 1L1‚Äč=1 and RM=NR_M = NRM‚Äč=N
Li=Ri‚ąí1+1L_i = R_{i - 1} + 1Li‚Äč=Ri‚ąí1‚Äč+1 for all 2‚Č§i‚Č§M2 \le i \le M2‚Č§i‚Č§M
Ri‚ąíLi+1‚Č§KR_i - L_i + 1 \le KRi‚Äč‚ąíLi‚Äč+1‚Č§K


For each i=1‚Ä¶Mi = 1 \ldots Mi=1‚Ä¶M, sort A[Li,Ri]A[L_i, R_i]A[Li‚Äč,Ri‚Äč] (i.e. replace the subarray with the sorted version of it) where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the subarray [AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äč].
Less formally, you divide the array into several subarrays, each of length at most KKK, and then sort each part and concatenate the pieces back together.

Define f(A)f(A)f(A) as the lexicographically minimum array possible at the end of the above process.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. It is guaranteed that AAA is a permutation of integers in [1,N][1, N][1,N].

Solve QQQ queries of the following type:


You are given 333 integers, LLL, RRR and XXX.

Let B=A[L,R]B = A[L, R]B=A[L,R] and C=f(B)C = f(B)C=f(B). Find the XXX-th element in CCC, i.e. CXC_XCX‚Äč.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 333 integers - N,KN, KN,K and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The next QQQ lines each contain 333 integers each - L,RL, RL,R and XXX representing a query.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ integers on the same line - the answers to the queries in the same order.
constraints: Ai‚Č†AjA_i \ne A_jAi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Aj‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N
The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ both do not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
